title: Add compact practice dashboard
goal: Add a data-driven practice dashboard to the homepage local-state area so returning users can see progress, active work, and the best continuation target without reintroducing the rejected quick-start/onboarding panel.
acceptance: Homepage local-state UI contains a clearly labeled Practice dashboard region with compact stats derived from browser localStorage: solved/submitted katas, active drafts or sessions, recent activity count, and a primary continue/start action.
Fresh browsers render a quiet zero-state that does not look like a marketing/onboarding block and does not add a large new section above the Library.
Seeded localStorage for kata results, drafts/sessions, and cursus progress changes the dashboard counts and primary Continue link deterministically.
Dashboard logic has unit coverage at a non-DOM seam or focused component tests, including malformed localStorage handling.
Playwright smoke coverage verifies the dashboard zero-state and a seeded returning-user state.
Do not introduce authentication, server persistence, telemetry, external services, private branding, or the previous quick-start card layout.
must_ask_if: You think the dashboard needs product copy or a large onboarding section rather than compact local progress UI.
You need to change storage key formats in a way that would break existing drafts, results, sessions, or cursus progress.
